content: The intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) that drive autonomous agent exploration can be repurposed as a dynamic pruning gate for cutting-edge active learning agent memory systems, where only unexpected, unpredicted state transitions that trigger curiosity are encoded into long-term memory instead of prioritizing memory storage by task reward alone. This framework aligns memory allocation with the agent’s long-term active learning goals, avoiding the common bloat and interference issues that plague modern large-scale agent memory.
